articles.title: Key Takeaways from Trump's China Visit & Fed's Iran-Tied Inflation Woes
articles.excerpt: President Trump and Chinese president Xi Jinping wrap up their meeting in China. Kevin Hincks highlights the key takeaways from the summit, from a big sale of Boeing (BA) planes to China, to discussions surrounding the Strait of Hormuz and crude oil.

articles.title: U.S. Industrial Production Rose in April
articles.excerpt: U.S. industrial production rose by 0.7% last month, after decreasing by an upwardly revised 0.3% in March.

articles.title: Why Soaring Treasury Yields Are Spooking the Stock Market
articles.excerpt: Rising Treasury yields are pressuring global stocks and forcing investors to rethink expectations for inflation and Federal Reserve interest-rate cuts.

articles.title: Saying goodbye to Powell's purple tie. 💜
articles.excerpt: Today is Jerome Powell's last day as the chair of the Federal Reserve. He'll continue to serve as a board governor.

articles.title: Factor Extremes: Momentum Runs Hot As Low-Vol Stumbles
articles.excerpt: The US stock market surged to yet another record high on Thursday, a new milestone that suggests a rising tide is lifting all equity sectors. Despite the headline strength in equities, the widening gap between factor winners and laggards underscores how uneven the market's intern

articles.title: Dow slides 400 points as rising yields and oil prices hit tech stocks
articles.excerpt: US stocks fell sharply on Friday as rising Treasury yields, persistent inflation concerns, and escalating energy prices pressured markets following another record-setting session for major indexes. The Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped roughly 402 points, or about 0.8%, while

articles.title: US manufacturing output accelerates on auto production in April
articles.excerpt: U.S. factory production accelerated in ​April as motor ‌vehicle output surged, suggesting underlying strength in ​manufacturing, though supply ​disruptions from the war ⁠with Iran are ​casting a shadow over ​the sector.

articles.title: Tech CEOs summoned to Capitol for June hearing, Axios reports
articles.excerpt: The CEOs ​of ‌Meta , Alphabet , ​TikTok ​and Snap have ⁠been ​invited ​back to Capitol ​Hill ​for a broad ‌oversight ⁠hearing in June, ​Axios ​reported ⁠on ​Friday.

articles.title: The Hindenburg Omen Flashes A Warning Sign
articles.excerpt: Recent S&P 500 and Nasdaq highs are driven by a narrowing group of stocks, raising concerns about market breadth. Strong Q1 corporate profits, robust job growth, and resilient consumer spending signal current economic health but may reflect past momentum.

articles.title: Prediction markets see surge in suspicious trades as popularity explodes
articles.excerpt: Top prediction market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket have witnessed a surge in suspicious trades this year, highlighting the popularity of the models with investors but causing a challenge to the platforms as they face increased scrutiny.

articles.title: Big Tech, Chips Will Push Nasdaq to 30,000, Ives Says
articles.excerpt: Dan Ives of Wedbush Securities says the Cerebras Systems IPO is a watershed moment for the tech sector. He sees the Nasdaq hitting 30,000 in the next six to nine months.

articles.title: Why the CLARITY Act's Bipartisan Support Could Change Everything for Bitcoin and XRP This May
articles.excerpt: The crypto market scored a major win on May 14 as the CLARITY Act cleared the Senate Banking Committee after months of debate over its provisions.

articles.title: Gemini stock surges despite lawsuit and restructuring concerns
articles.excerpt: Shares of Gemini Space Station surged more than 20% in premarket trading on Friday after the cryptocurrency exchange reported a smaller-than-expected quarterly loss and announced a $100 million investment from its founders. The New York-based company's shares, which were priced a

articles.title: Dow 50,000
articles.excerpt: The Dow Jones Industrial Average needed 76 years to climb its first 1,000 points. It needed 21 months to climb its last 10,000.

articles.title: Japan: I See An Opportunity
articles.excerpt: I am bullish on Japanese equities, emphasizing that Japan's high debt/GDP is offset by a robust asset base and pension fund scale. Japan's Government Pension Investment Fund and BOJ asset accumulation provide significant competitive advantages and underpin market resilience.

articles.title: Stock Market Pulls Back from Red-Hot Rally, CBRS Slides After Strong IPO
articles.excerpt: Markets wanted more from the meeting between President Trump and Chinese president Xi Jinping, says Alex Coffey. He talks about the "warm words" between the global leaders and what it means for Wall Street as it steps down from a stunning tech-led rally over recent weeks.

articles.title: The Stock Market Rally Faces a Stark Reality. Things Could Get Tricky.
articles.excerpt: Bonds, options and oil all suggest the recent tech-led rally might struggle to stay on the rails.

articles.title: Nasdaq set for cautious open after record-breaking Thursday as inflation and geopolitics weigh
articles.excerpt: 8am: Stocks set for pullback US stock futures are pointing to a softer open on Friday, with investors taking a step back after a barnstorming session on Thursday that saw both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq close at fresh record highs and the Dow Jones reclaim the psychologically importa

articles.title: Morning Call Sheet: AI rally masks growing cracks beneath markets
articles.excerpt: Peter Boockvar of One Point BFG, Henrietta Treyz of Veda Partners and Jay Woods of Freedom Capital Markets discuss AI momentum, rising yields, tariffs and growing market risks.

articles.title: The U.S. dollar has barely budged this year — but that could be about to change
articles.excerpt: Currency traders are not convinced that high interest rates are here to stay.
